subject: social science
text: Write a note on the Indian subcontinent.
key_answer: "Mother and Motherland are greater than Heaven." India's respect for its motherland is universal. India has been referred to as a subcontinent since ancient times, encompassing several countries in South Asia. India has contributed to mathematics, science, yoga, and culture. It's the birthplace of several religions. Ancient scientists like Aryabhata and Baudhayana contributed to science. According to mythology, the land ruled by King Bharata, son of Vrishabhanatha, was called "Bharatakhanda" or "Bharatavarsha". British scholars introduced India's antiquity to the world by translating several Indian texts into English. Hinduism spread to other countries from India. India advocates non-violence, with "Ahimsa Paramo Dharmah" meaning non-violence is the highest dharma. Indian values showcase its greatness. Regardless of faith or religion, the Vedic saying emphasizes that there's one God with many names. UNESCO recognizes and conserves India's ancient temples as World Heritage sites, making India exceptional globally.
